_doc: Calibration set for the L2 ABSTAIN gate (kleos-lib/tests/abstain_calibration.rs). Each query is labelled `answerable` (a relevant memory exists in the store) or `unanswerable` (none does). `sem_top`/`ce_top` are the best semantic_score (raw cosine) and best ce_confidence (normalized cross-encoder) a real retrieval produced for that query. The harness sweeps the semantic threshold through the production `evaluate_signals` logic and reports false-abstain rate (FAR) and true-abstain rate (TAR), asserting FAR<5%% / TAR>80%% at the shipped default (0.65). PROVENANCE: RE-CAPTURED 2026-06-28 from an isolated kleos-server (real bge-m3 embedder + bge-reranker-v2-m3 ONNX reranker, scratch DB) running each `q` against the `corpus` -- these are live signals, not seeds. bge-reranker-v2-m3 produces a sharply bimodal cross-encoder distribution (relevant pairs ~0.98-1.0, irrelevant ~0.00) vs granites compressed 0.5-0.9, so the CE rescue floor was recalibrated 0.80 -> 0.70 (the old floor wrongly dropped ans-dahlia-rescued at ce=0.752). A query that returns ZERO results (sem_top=null AND ce_top=null, e.g. un-atlantis) abstains in production via results.is_empty(); the harness mirrors that. Before freezing for a NEW embedder/reranker, re-capture and re-run the sweep. `corpus` exists for that live re-capture. DECEPTIVE CASES (2026-06-28): un-calyx-pg-version / un-cobalt-cost / un-vexel-port are unanswerable queries topically adjacent to a corpus doc (high cosine) whose specific fact no doc states (low CE); they exercise the ce_floor abstain path that semantic-only misses.

corpus.content: Calyx stores all primary state in PostgreSQL with a Redis cache layer
The Borealis project is led by engineering lead Marin Okafor
The Cobalt service migrated to its v2 storage backend on 2026-02-14
We chose Rust for the engine because of memory safety and predictable latency under load
Vexel uses SQLite with the sqlite-vec extension for its embedded vector store
My preferred editor is Helix with the catppuccin theme
Kleos listens on port 4200 by default
We run the BAAI/bge-m3 embedding model at 1024 dimensions
For German retrieval the HTTP reranker with bge-reranker-v2-m3 outperforms the English Granite cross-encoder
Dahlia is paused pending a security review of its ingest path
